channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Cross Browser Testing: A Complete Guide to Website Compatibility (2026)
item.description: A website that looks perfect in one browser might appear completely broken in another. Variations in rendering engines, JavaScript execution, and CSS interpretation mean that web applications can...

item.title: Di era AI, yang menang bukan yang kerja paling banyak
item.description: Saya mulai dari pertanyaan yang kelihatannya sederhana: mana yang lebih kuat, motivasi, disiplin, konsistensi, atau obsesi? Saya kira jawabannya salah satu dari empat itu. Ternyata bukan. Setelah...

item.title: Best AI Test Case Generation Tools (2026 Guide)
item.description: Writing test cases has always been the part of QA work that eats the most time without producing the most value. Translating a requirements document into structured test steps, covering edge...

item.title: The Journey of Finding Mentors
item.description: Have you ever met someone who challenged you in a way that helped you grow? During my undergrad, mentorship was not something I intentionally searched for or even fully understood. Nobody ever sat...

item.title: Serving 78 cards x 5 languages from one codebase: our i18n routing
item.description: Tarotas is a tarot card app we build at Inithouse. You shuffle, draw a card, and read a calm interpretation. No account required, no fortune-telling claims. Just a quiet space for reflection. The...

item.title: Building a cross-user review graph with pgvector on Amazon Aurora
item.description: Every review app is a silo. Yelp reviews places, Amazon reviews its own catalog, Letterboxd reviews film. I wanted to build the opposite: one place where you can log and rate anything — a...

item.title: Serving cheap when two models agree: a measured cost lever
item.description: The problem A cost efficient AI system sends easy work to a cheap model and only escalates hard work to an expensive frontier model. The trouble is knowing which is which. When a task has a test,...

item.title: The Unicode trap I had to design around in a LinkedIn formatter
item.description: I built a small tool for formatting LinkedIn posts, and the deceptively simple part was also the most important part: styled text is not formatting. It is Unicode substitution. That sounds obvious...

item.title: AI SDK 7: Agent Development Standardized
item.description: The theme this week is consolidation: AI SDK 7 ships a provider-agnostic foundation for production agents, LangSmith closes the loop on observability with a purpose-built database and on-call...

item.title: Building an AI Cloud Cost Intelligence Platform That Doesn't Let AI Make Infrastructure Decisions
item.description: Most AI-powered cloud optimization demos follow a simple approach: Azure Resources ↓ Large Language Model ↓ Recommendations At first glance, this seems impressive. But while building my own Azure...

item.title: China’s Z.ai claims it can match Mythos on cybersecurity
item.description: China's Zhipu AI (Z.ai) released its open-weight GLM-5.2, and some researchers have claimed that it matches Mythos in certain bug-finding and cybersecurity scenarios. While GLM lags behind models...

item.title: Suno launches Spark incubator program to feed independent artists to its AI machine
item.description: Suno has ambitions to be more than just a toy to churn out AI slop, it also wants to be a streaming destination and to break new artists. Spark is their new incubator program for independent...

item.title: Ford rehires ‘gray beard’ engineers after AI falls short
item.description: "Mistakenly we thought that by just introducing artificial intelligence ... that would produce a high-quality product.”

item.title: China claims the world’s fastest supercomputer
item.description: Despite trade restrictions, China has reclaimed the title of the world's fastest supercomputer for the first time since 2018. LineShine has pushed El Capitan out of number one on the TOP500...

item.title: HP Inc. launches Frontier strategic partnership with OpenAI
item.description: HP Inc. scales its OpenAI Frontier partnership to deploy AI across customer experiences, software development, and enterprise operations.

item.title: The Cube is Jim Henson’s little-known proto-Black Mirror masterpiece
item.description: I'm sure we're all familiar with Dark Crystal, so we know that Jim Henson can be weird and tackle slightly more mature subject matter. But there is little in his oeuvre that is quite as...

item.title: Why Wall Street thinks US memory maker Micron is the next Nvidia
item.description: Eager to find more public AI-related companies that may do as well as Nvidia, Wall Street investors think they've found a winner with Micron.

item.title: Prosecutors used ChatGPT logs as evidence in the Palisades fire trial
item.description: Jonathan Rinderknecht was facing arson charges for setting a fire on New Year's Day in 2025, which became one of the deadliest wildfires in LA history. To make their case, prosecutors turned to...

item.title: Nest’s quest to fix your thermostat
item.description: The founding story of Nest is pretty much a perfect tech myth. A legendary product maker (in this case, Tony Fadell) helps create one of the most successful products ever (the iPhone) and then...

item.title: Ad-free streaming is a luxury now
item.description: This is The Stepback, a weekly newsletter breaking down one essential story from the tech world. For more news about the streaming industry, follow Emma Roth. The Stepback arrives in our...

item.title: TMD’s keyless bike lock is a $280 solution to a $60 problem
item.description: I've seen lots of so-called "smart" bike locks over the years, but none so far could justify the added cost. A newcomer that got its start securing ATMs for banks is trying to change that. There's...

item.title: Teenage Engineering adds lo-fi mode, USB audio, and more to its KO II sampler
item.description: Teenage Engineering has already issued multiple substantial updates for its surprisingly capable $329 EP-133 KO II sampler. Its latest is one of the biggest yet. OS 2.5 adds audio over USB,...

item.title: SoftBank’s CEO isn’t the only one with questions about Elon Musk’s orbital data center hype
item.description: Not everyone is buying Elon Musk’s vision for orbital data centers.

item.title: Margaret Atwood says the problem with AI is ‘garbage in, garbage out’
item.description: Maraget Atwood, the storied author of The Handmaid's Tale and The Blind Assassin, was interviewed as part of the Babell Literary and Cultural Festival in Porto, Portugal. As it usually does at...

item.title: Ukraine Says Russian Intelligence Used Fake Support Texts to Steal Messaging Credentials
item.description: The Security Service of Ukraine (SSU) said it, together with the U.S. Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI), uncovered a long-running campaign orchestrated by Russian intelligence services to...

item.title: Apple Vision Pro exec is reportedly leaving for OpenAI
item.description: Paul Meade, the Apple vice president in charge of the Vision Pro headset, is reportedly leaving the company to join OpenAI’s hardware team.

item.title: The fittest founder in the room got cancer. Here’s how he used AI to fight back.
item.description: When confronted with cancer, Connor Christou fed everything tied tied to his regime — blood results, scan data, wearable output, journal entries — into Claude.

item.title: OpenAI Previews GPT-5.6 Sol With Restricted Access and Stronger Cyber Safeguards
item.description: OpenAI on Friday released three versions of GPT-5.6, called Sol, Terra, and Luna, as a limited preview to a small number of companies as part of an ongoing engagement with the U.S. government....

item.title: Asian AI startups launch Mythos-like models as Anthropic’s export ban drags on
item.description: New models are launching in Asia that promise Mythos-like capabilities without fear of an export ban. U.S. AI labs may never recover this enormous market.

item.title: Trump Admin releases Anthropic Mythos to be used by more than 100 US companies, agencies
item.description: Over 100 companies and government agencies are reportedly authorized to use Mythos 5, including their non-American employees.
